description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a slightly tapered profile, wider at the top and narrower at the base. Its surface is composed of multiple horizontal layers, each resembling individual stone bricks, giving it a blocky, modular appearance. The stone texture is rough and uneven, with visible variations in shading and subtle surface irregularities that mimic natural stone. The sides are relatively thin compared to the front and back, emphasizing its slab-like form. The coloration is predominantly gray, with darker and lighter patches that enhance the sense of depth and realism. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering, suggesting it is either new or well-maintained.
description: A gray, rectangular stone block with a layered, brick-like texture and a slightly tapered shape. The surface is rough and natural-looking, with no visible markings or weathering.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width and height of the stone block, highlighting the layered, brick-like texture and the slight taper from top to bottom.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin profile of the block, with the layered texture visible along the edge.
description_view_2: Back view is nearly identical to the front, displaying the same brick-like pattern and overall shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow thickness and the rough stone texture along the edge.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or boundary barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
